Web crawl, scrape, extract, embed, and query -- all in one binary backed by a self-hosted RAG stack Axon is a trimodal application: |------|-------------|------|---------| | CLI | `axon <command>` | -- | Interactive command-line tool for crawl, scrape, embed, query, ask | | MCP server | `axon mcp` | 8001 | Single-tool MCP server exposing all CLI operations to AI agents | | Web panel + HTTP API | `axon serve` | 8001 | Unified HTTP server for web panel, MCP, `/v1/ask`, and `/v1/actions` | All three modes share the same Rust binary, the same services layer, and the same infrastructure stack |------|-------------| |------|-------------| |------|-------------| | [rep

← Business logic layer (dep: all above) ├── axon-mcp/ ← MCP server, schema, OAuth (dep: axon-core, axon-services) ├── axon-web/ ← Axum server, REST API, WebSocket (dep: axon-core, axon-services) └── axon-cli/ ← CLI command handlers, thin dispatch (dep: axon-services, axon-mcp, axon-web) bin/ ├── axon/ ← Main binary (dep: axon-cli) └── axon-client/ ← Thin remote client (dep: axon-core ONLY — no infra deps) apps/ └── web/ ← Next js (unchanged) ``` ``` axon-core ├── axon-crawl ├── axon-vector ├── axon-ingest (core + vector) ├── axon-jobs (core + crawl + vector + ingest) ├── axon-services (core + crawl + vector + jobs + ingest) ├── axon-mcp (core + services) ├── axon-web (core + services) └── axon-cli (services + mcp + web) └── bin/axon bin/axon-client (core ONLY) ``` The compiler enforces this DAG — circular deps are compile errors ```toml [workspace] resolver = "2" members = [ "crates/axon-core", "crates/axon-crawl", "crates/axon-vector", "crates/axon-jobs", "crates/axon-ingest", "crates/axon-services", "crates/axon-mcp", "crates/axon-web", "crates/axon-cli", "bin/axon", "bin/axon-client", ] [workspace

Web crawl, scrape, extract, embed, and query — all in one binary backed by a self-hosted RAG stack Axon is a trimodal application: |------|-------------|------|---------| | CLI | `axon <command>` | — | Interactive command-line tool for crawl, scrape, summarize, embed, query, ask | | MCP server | `axon mcp` | 8001 | Single-tool MCP server exposing all CLI operations to AI agents | | Web panel + HTTP API | `axon serve` | 8001 | Unified HTTP server for web panel, MCP, and direct `/v1` REST routes | All three modes share the same Rust binary, the same services layer, and the same infrastructure stack Living/reference docs are grouped by intent

1 Axon is a self-hosted RAG stack for crawling, scraping, ingesting, embedding, searching, and asking questions over indexed content The production release is Docker Compose first: one Axon server container, Qdrant, Hugging Face TEI with `Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-0 cpp are supported when configured with `AXON_LLM_BACKEND=openai-compat` CLI and MCP run all actions in-process; deploy the `axon serve` container only when you need HTTP API access Not supported in the production path: systemd deployment of the Axon binary

Step-by-step instructions to get Axon running locally or via Docker ```bash git clone ~/workspace/axon cd ~/workspace/axon ``` ```bash /scripts/axon setup init ``` This creates or refreshes `~/ `setup init` defaults to loopback MCP HTTP, writes `AXON_MCP_AUTH_MODE=bearer`, and generates `AXON_MCP_HTTP_TOKEN` | | OAuth MCP auth | `AXON_MCP_PUBLIC_URL`, `AXON_MCP_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `AXON_MCP_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, and `AXON_MCP_AUTH_ADMIN_EMAIL`

yaml ps axon preflight axon doctor ``` Stop: ```bash docker compose --env-file ~/ yaml down ``` Development stack: ```bash cargo build --bin axon docker compose --env-file yaml build axon docker compose --env-file ~/ yaml up -d axon ``` The development stack uses the production infrastructure definitions but runs `axon` from the bind-mounted local debug binary under `target/debug`, inside the newer `axon:dev-runtime` image Axon has two config layers: Precedence: ```text CLI flags > environment variables > ~/

env ``` `axon setup init` is non-destructive: it adds missing required runtime keys and fills blank generated auth tokens, but it does not prune unknown keys If `AXON_ENV_FILE` is set, Axon treats that file as the effective env file `AXON_SERVER_URL` is the generic client/server switch for the CLI When it is set, supported stateful commands call a running `axon serve` HTTP endpoint instead of executing locally: ```bash AXON_SERVER_URL=http://127 1:8001 axon status --json AXON_SERVER_URL=http://127

Step-by-step instructions to get Axon running locally or via Docker ```bash git clone ~/workspace/axon_rust cd ~/workspace/axon_rust ``` ```bash env` and set required values: ```bash AXON_HOME=/home/you/ 1:52000 ``` Optional client/server mode for host CLI calls: ```bash AXON_SERVER_URL=http://127 1:8001 AXON_MCP_HTTP_TOKEN= ``` See CONFIG

Start the stack: **Infrastructure only** (Qdrant, TEI, Chrome in Docker; `axon serve` run locally): ```bash docker compose --env-file ~/ yaml up -d axon-qdrant axon-tei axon-chrome ``` To run the `axon` service through Compose, start the full stack: ```bash docker compose --env-file ~/ yaml up -d ``` The Compose `axon` service publishes MCP HTTP via `${AXON_MCP_HTTP_PUBLISH:-8001}:8001` env` to restrict it to loopback, and ensure `AXON_MCP_HTTP_TOKEN` is set (or OAuth mode enabled) whenever the port is exposed beyond loopback **Local dev mode** (infra in Docker, Axon server run locally): ```bash docker compose --env-file ~/

Check connectivity to all required services mcp Start MCP stdio or unified HTTP runtime serve Start service runtimes setup Initialize and inspect Axon infrastructure preflight Check host prerequisites and service readiness smoke Run crawl/ask smoke checks against the running stack compose Manage the local Docker Compose service stack completions Generate shell completions (bash, zsh, fish) config Read or write entries in ~/ toml → Run axon <command> --help for command-specific flags

Key Source Map This document defines the current architecture of `axon` across: CLI command execution and dispatch Crawl/extract/embed/ingest asynchronous pipelines Vector storage and retrieval (Qdrant + TEI) Unified HTTP runtime (`axon serve` web panel, MCP, `/v1/ask`, and direct `/v1` REST routes) CLI client/server data flow It supersedes the previous architecture notes for the removed omnibox/Pulse web runtime ```mermaid flowchart LR U[User or API client] CLI[axon CLI binary] QD[(Qdrant)] TEI[TEI embeddings] LLM[LLM completion backend: Gemini headless default, OpenAI-compatible optional] CHR[Chrome/CDP] SQ[(SQLite jobs)] U --> CLI CLI --> QD CLI --> TEI CLI --> LLM CLI --> CHR CLI --> SQ ``` |---|---|

Axon uses two user-editable files under `~/ Use `QDRANT_URL`, `TEI_URL`, `AXON_SERVER_URL`, and `AXON_CHROME_REMOTE_URL` from the env layer axon/` is the canonical home for all Axon user-level config, secrets, runtime state, infrastructure data, and generated output All app data lives directly under this directory — no nested `axon/` subdirectory env # URLs + secrets (loaded after AXON_ENV_FILE, │ # before repo-root

— warns if collection is unnamed but `AXON_HYBRID_SEARCH=true` `axon status` reports per-kind job counts (Crawl / Extract / Embed / Ingest) and recent jobs (top 10) For Docker Compose operation, keep `axon serve` as the authoritative process and point host CLI calls at it: ```bash AXON_SERVER_URL=http://127 1:8001 axon status --json AXON_SERVER_URL=http://127 1:8001 axon scrape --json AXON_SERVER_URL=http://127 1:8001 axon crawl ``` In server mode, stateful commands run on the server and the server owns SQLite job rows, markdown output, screenshots, and artifacts under its `AXON_DATA_DIR` (default `~/

com/callback/* ``` OAuth mode also accepts `AXON_MCP_HTTP_TOKEN` when set, so existing bearer clients can continue working while OAuth clients use dynamic registration and JWT bearer tokens OAuth/JWT callers are scope-checked by MCP action: write actions require an Axon write scope and read actions require an Axon read scope, but `scope_satisfies` in `src/authz rs` treats either Axon scope (`axon:read` or `axon:write`) as satisfying any Axon-scoped action, so a token holding either scope reaches all Axon read and write routes (`migrate`/`dedupe` require an Axon scope as well; unknown actions fail closed)
